text:
Market today: Chip stocks are bouncing today and Alphabet is up 4% on its Dow debut - risk appetite is back on in tech. That chip rebound is the key driver; anything semiconductor-related should catch a bid.

Your holdings: AVGO (-14.23%) is your biggest problem - largest position, already down hard, and on margin that pain is real; the TRIM signal means start reducing, not hoping. PLTR (-10.52%) has a SELL signal - you're underwater and the scanner wants you out before it gets worse. PTNM (-15.25%) is a tiny spec already near its $9.50 stop - easiest cut you can make today. SPCX is a brand-new, very volatile IPO with thin history; margin amplifies any sudden drop fast.

Watch: Does the chip rebound have legs - watch SMH holding above $631. Also watch AVGO specifically for any bounce you can use to trim size.

Bottom line: Stop hoping on PLTR and AVGO - you're on margin, losses compound, and the scanner is telling you the same thing.
